content:
# pattern-role-based-agent-review-gates - Overview

Workflow type: review_gate
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, opencode, Cursor, OpenClaw
Goal: Prevent AI agents from skipping design and jumping directly to code by inserting role-based review gates at Plan, Review, Test, and Ship stages with defined checklists, stop conditions, and escalation paths.
Evidence: documented / high
Source: https://github.com/garrytan/gstack

When to use:
- Before any Build phase starts (Plan gates)
- After code is written and before merging (Review gates)
- Before deployment (Test gates)
- Before release (Ship gates)

Inputs required:
- Project type classification (web/backend/automation/desktop)
- Risk level assessment (authentication? payments? user data?)
- Available review resources (single model or cross-model)

AI summary: Role-based review gates for AI agent development: Plan gates (CEO/Eng/Design/DX) prevent skipping design, Review gates (Code/Design/DX/Security) catch bugs, Test gates (QA/Security/Performance) verify quality, Ship gates ensure safe deployment. Failed gates send work back to earlier stages.

# pattern-role-based-agent-review-gates - Execution Steps

Workflow type: review_gate
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, opencode, Cursor, OpenClaw
Goal: Prevent AI agents from skipping design and jumping directly to code by inserting role-based review gates at Plan, Review, Test, and Ship stages with defined checklists, stop conditions, and escalation paths.
Evidence: documented / high
Source: https://github.com/garrytan/gstack

- Plan Gates: CEO (scope) → Eng (architecture) → Design (UI projects) → DX (API projects)
- Review Gates: Code Review → Design Review (UI changes) → DX Review (API changes) → Security Review (high-risk)
- Test Gates: QA (browser for web) → Security Scan (high-risk) → Performance Benchmark (as needed)
- Ship Gates: Ship checklist → Canary Monitor (production) → Document Release
- Failed gates → back to appropriate earlier stage for remediation

AI summary: Role-based review gates for AI agent development: Plan gates (CEO/Eng/Design/DX) prevent skipping design, Review gates (Code/Design/DX/Security) catch bugs, Test gates (QA/Security/Performance) verify quality, Ship gates ensure safe deployment. Failed gates send work back to earlier stages.

# pattern-role-based-agent-review-gates - Prompt Contract

Workflow type: review_gate
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, opencode, Cursor, OpenClaw
Goal: Prevent AI agents from skipping design and jumping directly to code by inserting role-based review gates at Plan, Review, Test, and Ship stages with defined checklists, stop conditions, and escalation paths.
Evidence: documented / high
Source: https://github.com/garrytan/gstack

- Agent must not enter Build without passing required Plan gates
- Cross-model agreement is signal, not proof — user has final say
- Gates are sequential within each stage; all required gates must pass
- Skipping gates requires explicit user override

AI summary: Role-based review gates for AI agent development: Plan gates (CEO/Eng/Design/DX) prevent skipping design, Review gates (Code/Design/DX/Security) catch bugs, Test gates (QA/Security/Performance) verify quality, Ship gates ensure safe deployment. Failed gates send work back to earlier stages.

# pattern-role-based-agent-review-gates - Verification

Workflow type: review_gate
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, opencode, Cursor, OpenClaw
Goal: Prevent AI agents from skipping design and jumping directly to code by inserting role-based review gates at Plan, Review, Test, and Ship stages with defined checklists, stop conditions, and escalation paths.
Evidence: documented / high
Source: https://github.com/garrytan/gstack

- Count of required gates equals count of executed gates
- No P0 issues remain from any gate
- Gate results documented with evidence

AI summary: Role-based review gates for AI agent development: Plan gates (CEO/Eng/Design/DX) prevent skipping design, Review gates (Code/Design/DX/Security) catch bugs, Test gates (QA/Security/Performance) verify quality, Ship gates ensure safe deployment. Failed gates send work back to earlier stages.

# pattern-role-based-agent-review-gates - Failure Modes

Workflow type: review_gate
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, opencode, Cursor, OpenClaw
Goal: Prevent AI agents from skipping design and jumping directly to code by inserting role-based review gates at Plan, Review, Test, and Ship stages with defined checklists, stop conditions, and escalation paths.
Evidence: documented / high
Source: https://github.com/garrytan/gstack

- Agent ignores gate failure and proceeds anyway
- Applying all gates to trivial changes (overhead)
- Skipping Security Gate for projects with auth/payment/user data

AI summary: Role-based review gates for AI agent development: Plan gates (CEO/Eng/Design/DX) prevent skipping design, Review gates (Code/Design/DX/Security) catch bugs, Test gates (QA/Security/Performance) verify quality, Ship gates ensure safe deployment. Failed gates send work back to earlier stages.

# pattern-role-based-agent-review-gates - Handoff Report

Workflow type: review_gate
Agent targets: Claude Code, Codex, opencode, Cursor, OpenClaw
Goal: Prevent AI agents from skipping design and jumping directly to code by inserting role-based review gates at Plan, Review, Test, and Ship stages with defined checklists, stop conditions, and escalation paths.
Evidence: documented / high
Source: https://github.com/garrytan/gstack

- List of executed gates with pass/fail status
- Remaining P0/P1 issues from each gate
- Which gates were skipped and why

AI summary: Role-based review gates for AI agent development: Plan gates (CEO/Eng/Design/DX) prevent skipping design, Review gates (Code/Design/DX/Security) catch bugs, Test gates (QA/Security/Performance) verify quality, Ship gates ensure safe deployment. Failed gates send work back to earlier stages.
